title: when the go back to all actions is done from nurse view in quick log, the oth...
body:
## 🐛 Bug Report

when the go back to all actions is done from nurse view in quick log, the other nurse position is off. It should intuitively provide info to switch on the other action

---

**Category:** `bug` `content`
**Source:** Telegram Bot
**Status:** Pending implementation

> _This issue was created automatically via the BabyBloom Telegram Bot. No private baby data is included._
enhanced_description:
BUG: When a breast-feeding timer is active (e.g. Nurse Left) and the user taps "← All actions" to return to the quick log grid, the OTHER breast button (e.g. Nurse Right) appears fully disabled (opacity 0.35, no click handler). This is confusing because the user cannot tell they can switch sides from the grid view.

- Affected component: src/tabs/HomeTab.tsx — quick log grid button definitions (lines ~1555-1556) and grid rendering (lines ~2046-2077)
- Current behaviour: qlBreastL/qlBreastR set dis=true when the OTHER breast timer is active, making the button greyed out and unclickable
- Expected behaviour: When one breast timer is active, the other breast button should become a "Switch" action (not disabled) — tapping it calls switchFeedSide() to switch to that side, with a visual indicator (e.g. label changes to "Switch Right" or shows a switch icon)
- Root cause: Line 1555-1556 — dis: feedTimer && feedTimer.type !== 'Breast L' unconditionally disables the non-active breast button
- Edge cases: (1) Only apply switch behavior when timerCollapsed is true (grid visible); (2) Preserve disabled state for non-breast timers like Tummy Time; (3) Ensure the switch button style is distinct from both active and normal states
- Acceptance criteria: When nursing on one side and viewing the all-actions grid, the other breast button is tappable, visually distinct (not greyed out), and calls switchFeedSide() to switch sides
- Testing: Add unit test verifying that when a breast timer is active, the other breast button is not disabled and triggers switchFeedSide; add test that non-breast timers still disable both breast buttons correctly
implementation_notes:
Files changed:
- src/tabs/HomeTab.tsx: Changed qlBreastL/qlBreastR definitions so that when the OTHER breast timer is active, the button becomes a switch action (label: "Switch Left"/"Switch Right", emoji: 🔄, fn: switchFeedSide) instead of being disabled. Added switchHint styling flag for distinct visual appearance (C.sl background, C.s text). Added sortKey field for stable sorting when label changes. Updated pinning logic to handle both original and switch labels. Added qlCategoryInfo entries for Switch Left/Right so long-press info panel works.

Tests added:
- tests/unit/nurseSwitchBtn.test.ts: 11 tests covering switch label, switch function, emoji change, disabled-only-for-non-breast timers, switchHint styling, sortKey, pinning logic, and category info aliases.

Approach: Instead of disabling the other breast button entirely, we detect when the other breast is active and transform the button into a switch action. The button stays visually distinct (secondary color scheme) and calls switchFeedSide() on tap. Non-breast timers (e.g. Tummy Time) still correctly disable both breast buttons.

title: when I upload my own image for hero widget, I would like to drag it a bit to ...
body:
## ✨ Feature Request

when I upload my own image for hero widget, I would like to drag it a bit to get a perfect fit myself.

---

**Category:** `enhancement`
**Source:** Telegram Bot
**Status:** Pending implementation

> _This issue was created automatically via the BabyBloom Telegram Bot. No private baby data is included._
enhanced_description:
User wants to reposition (drag/pan) their uploaded custom image within the hero widget to get a better crop/fit.

Component affected: Hero widget image upload/display (likely in src/ — the hero/banner component that shows a user-uploaded photo).

Current behaviour: When a user uploads a custom image for the hero widget, the image is displayed with a fixed crop/position. The user cannot adjust which part of the image is visible.

Expected behaviour: After uploading an image for the hero widget, the user should be able to drag/pan the image within the widget frame to choose the visible portion, achieving a perfect fit/crop.

Edge cases to handle:
- Image smaller than the widget frame (no drag needed or scale-to-fill first)
- Touch vs mouse interactions (this is primarily a mobile app, so touch drag)
- Persisting the chosen position (objectPosition or offset) so it survives page reloads
- Preventing drag from conflicting with page scroll
- Aspect ratios that differ significantly from the widget frame

Acceptance criteria:
1. After uploading a hero image, user can touch-drag to reposition it within the widget
2. The chosen position is saved and restored on reload
3. Works smoothly on mobile (touch events) with no scroll interference
4. A visual indicator shows the image is draggable (e.g., move cursor or subtle hint)

Testing:
- Unit test for position state persistence (save/load of image offset)
- Unit test for drag offset calculation logic
- Test that drag boundaries are respected (image cannot be dragged beyond its edges)
implementation_notes:
Files changed:
- src/features/settings/HeroBackgroundPicker.tsx: Added optional position field to HeroBgSetting interface, added clampPosition utility, added drag state management (touch + mouse), added drag-to-reposition preview UI below photo grid
- src/tabs/HomeTab.tsx: Updated photo background rendering to use saved position (backgroundPosition) with center fallback
- tests/unit/heroDarkMode.test.ts: Increased hero region read window from 1000 to 1200 chars (style block grew with new background-position properties)

Tests added:
- tests/unit/heroDragReposition.test.ts: 13 tests covering position field in interface, drag UI presence (touch/mouse handlers, touchAction:none), clampPosition logic, and HomeTab position application with center fallback

Approach:
- Added position?: {x,y} (percentages 0-100) to HeroBgSetting, defaulting to 50/50 (center)
- Drag preview uses touch + mouse events with touchAction:none to prevent scroll interference
- Position is saved to IndexedDB on drag end
- HomeTab applies position via backgroundPosition CSS property
